perturbation.original_text: For purposes of this Agreement "Net Revenue" means gross revenue recognized by Client on transactions conducted by users referred to the Client Site from the Excite Network minus sales tax, sales returns and allowances. Client will pay Excite these revenue share payments within thirty (30) days after the close of the financial quarter in which Client recognizes the Net Revenue on these transactions.
perturbation.changed_text: For purposes of this Agreement "Net Revenue" means gross revenue recognized by Client on transactions conducted by users referred to the Client Site from the Excite Network. Client will pay Excite these revenue share payments within thirty (30) days after the close of the financial quarter in which Client recognizes the Net Revenue on these transactions.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase "minus sales tax, sales returns and allowances" from the definition of "Net Revenue", the agreement introduces ambiguity regarding the calculation of revenue share payments. This omission creates an in-text contradiction because the subsequent sections refer to "Net Revenue" without clarifying whether deductions for sales tax, returns, and allowances are permissible, leading to potential disputes over the actual amount owed. This now creates ambiguity in how much has to be paid, since there will not be a proper deductions made, which is creating a contradiction in what Excite will receive as full payment.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Separate and apart from the sponsorship and advertising fees, the MatchLogic DeliverE fees and the MatchLogic banner and link serving fees, Client will pay Excite [*****] recognized by Client on transactions conducted by users referred to the Client Site from the Excite Network during [*****]. Separate and apart from the sponsorship and advertising fees, the MatchLogic DeliverE fees and the MatchLogic banner and link serving fees, Client will pay Excite [*****] recognized by Client on transactions conducted by users referred to the Client Site from the Excite Network during [*****] of the term of the Agreement. For purposes of this Agreement "Net Revenue" means gross revenue recognized by Client on transactions conducted by users referred to the Client Site from the Excite Network. Client will pay Excite these revenue share payments within thirty (30) days after the close of the financial quarter in which Client recognizes the Net Revenue on these transactions.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits "minus sales tax, sales returns and allowances" from the definition of "Net Revenue". The contradicted text references "Net Revenue" without specifying whether these deductions are permissible. The omission creates a direct conflict because it introduces ambiguity about the calculation of revenue share payments, contradicting the expectation of a clear definition of 'Net Revenue' as used in the contradicted section. This aligns with the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' as essential language was removed, creating a contradiction with obligations stated in other sections.

perturbation.original_text: Advertising banners will be served, tracked and reported by Excite's subsidiary, MatchLogic, Inc. ("MatchLogic") as described in Exhibit B. MatchLogic will also provide Client with feedback as to comparisons of the performance of (i) the different creative messages supplied by Client for the advertising banners, (ii) the placements of those advertising banners on the Excite Network as set forth in this Agreement and (iii) through the implementation of MatchLogic's Closed Loop transaction reporting system on the Client Site, will report on correlations between transaction activity by users referred to the Client Site from the Excite Network and the various promotional placements and advertising displayed on the Excite Network, all as described in Exhibit B. Promotional placements, including text links, will be served, tracked and reported by Excite. These promotional placements will be tracked and reported by MatchLogic when this implementation becomes available. Excite will provide Client with monthly reports substantiating the number of impressions of Client's advertising banners and promotional placements displayed on the Excite Network.
perturbation.changed_text: Advertising banners will be served, tracked and reported by Excite's subsidiary, MatchLogic, Inc. ("MatchLogic") as described in Exhibit B. MatchLogic will also provide Client with feedback as to comparisons of the performance of (i) the different creative messages supplied by Client for the advertising banners, (ii) the placements of those advertising banners on the Excite Network as set forth in this Agreement and (iii) through the implementation of MatchLogic's Closed Loop transaction reporting system on the Client Site, will report on correlations between transaction activity by users referred to the Client Site from the Excite Network and the various promotional placements and advertising displayed on the Excite Network, all as described in Exhibit B. These promotional placements will be tracked and reported by MatchLogic when this implementation becomes available. Excite will provide Client with monthly reports substantiating the number of impressions of Client's advertising banners and promotional placements displayed on the Excite Network.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the statement "Promotional placements, including text links, will be served, tracked and reported by Excite." there is now an ambiguity on who and how Promotional placements, including text links will be served and tracked. This creates an in-text contradiction because it's not clear who is responsible for serving and tracking that perticular information, creating a contradiction in who is responsible for the material.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Advertising banners will be served, tracked and reported by Excite's subsidiary, MatchLogic, Inc. ("MatchLogic") as described in Exhibit B. MatchLogic will also provide Client with feedback as to comparisons of the performance of (i) the different creative messages supplied by Client for the advertising banners, (ii) the placements of those advertising banners on the Excite Network as set forth in this Agreement and (iii) through the implementation of MatchLogic's Closed Loop transaction reporting system on the Client Site, will report on correlations between transaction activity by users referred to the Client Site from the Excite Network and the various promotional placements and advertising displayed on the Excite Network, all as described in Exhibit B. These promotional placements will be tracked and reported by MatchLogic when this implementation becomes available. Excite will provide Client with monthly reports substantiating the number of impressions of Client's advertising banners and promotional placements displayed on the Excite Network.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states "Promotional placements, including text links, will be served, tracked and reported by Excite.". The changed text omits this statement, creating ambiguity about who is responsible for these tasks, directly contradicting the implied assignment of responsibility elsewhere in the document, specifically as the omitted text directly contradicts "These promotional placements will be tracked and reported by MatchLogic when this implementation becomes available.", as it is not clear who is responsible for serving the placements.

perturbation.original_text: Client will retain all right, title and interest in and to the Client Site worldwide including all intellectual property rights, including but not limited to copyright, trademark, trade secrets, patents, moral rights or any derivative rights thereof. Any intellectual property rights, including but not limited to copyright, trademark, trade secrets, patents, moral rights or any derivative rights thereof, created by changes made by Excite to Impression Materials are the sole property of Client.
perturbation.changed_text: Client will retain all right, title and interest in and to the Client Site worldwide including all intellectual property rights, including but not limited to copyright, trademark, trade secrets, patents, moral rights or any derivative rights thereof.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the second sentence: "Any intellectual property rights, including but not limited to copyright, trademark, trade secrets, patents, moral rights or any derivative rights thereof, created by changes made by Excite to Impression Materials are the sole property of Client." from Section 11(a), this creates an in-text contradiction regarding the ownership of intellectual property rights related to changes made by Excite to the Impression Materials. If Excite modifies the impression material, which company will own them?
perturbation.contradicted_text: Client will retain all right, title and interest in and to the Client Site worldwide including all intellectual property rights, including but not limited to copyright, trademark, trade secrets, patents, moral rights or any derivative rights thereof.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly stated that the Client owned the intellectual property rights to changes made by Excite to the Impression Materials. The changed text omits this sentence, creating a contradiction. Now, it's unclear who owns the IP if Excite modifies the materials, directly conflicting with the implied assumption in the contradicted text that the Client owns all IP related to the Client Site, including changes to Impression Materials.
